title: Palmijuncus lewisianus
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Palmijuncus lewisianus. However, I found that the plant Lewisia (Lewisia lewisii) or Lewis's Coughwort (Lewisia rediviva) has some similar information that might fit your request.

Lewisia (Lewis's Coughwort) is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its unique, showy flowers and edible roots. The plant is valued for its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it beneficial in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs, bronchitis, and sore throats. The bioactive constituents of Lewisia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lewisia is commonly prepared as an herbal tea or tincture to help relieve respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
